semantic.role: Configuration file for code dependency analysis within ArchSpine
responsibilities: Defines export symbol patterns for classes, structs, enums, protocols, and functions
Defines usage patterns for function calls and method calls
outOfScope: Actual code analysis logic or execution
Support for languages other than Swift (based on patterns)

English.purpose: This document serves as a language configuration file for the ArchSpine mirror system, specifying how the tool recognizes imports, exported symbols, and usages in Swift source code. It allows the system to accurately map dependencies and call graphs.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers and maintainers of ArchSpine who need to customize or extend the code analysis engine. It is relevant when adding support for new Swift constructs or modifying detection patterns.
key_takeaways: The configuration uses YAML-like syntax to map human-readable patterns to internal identifiers.
Imports are detected by matching 'import <source>' lines.
Exports cover classes, structs, enums, protocols, and functions using corresponding Swift declaration keywords.
Usages are detected as direct function calls `name(args)` and method calls `object.method`.
This file is likely part of a larger set of language definitions that drive ArchSpine's dependency resolution.
